questions.question: Why are transport systems important for economic development?
questions.answer: They help move goods and people efficiently between production and consumption areas.

questions.question: What role does communication play in modern trade?
questions.answer: It helps exchange information quickly between producers, traders and consumers.

questions.question: Give one advantage of roadways over railways.
questions.answer: Roadways provide door-to-door transport services.

questions.question: Which authority manages National Highways in India?
questions.answer: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI).

questions.question: Why are Border Roads important?
questions.answer: They improve accessibility in remote areas and support defence activities.

questions.question: Why are railway networks dense in the Northern Plains?
questions.answer: Because the land is flat and densely populated.

questions.question: Name one difficulty in building railways in mountainous regions.
questions.answer: Steep slopes and rugged terrain.

questions.question: What products are commonly transported through pipelines?
questions.answer: Crude oil, petroleum products and natural gas.

questions.question: Why are inland waterways considered economical?
questions.answer: They can transport bulky goods at lower cost.

questions.question: Name the inland waterway on the Brahmaputra river.
questions.answer: National Waterway No.2.

questions.question: Which port handles a large share of container traffic in India?
questions.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru Port.

questions.question: Which port was developed to reduce congestion at Mumbai Port?
questions.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru Port.

questions.question: Why is air transport useful in mountainous areas?
questions.answer: It can reach remote areas where roads and railways are difficult to build.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the UDAN scheme?
questions.answer: To improve regional air connectivity at affordable prices.

questions.question: What is meant by mass communication?
questions.answer: Communication that reaches a large number of people through media.

questions.question: Name two means of mass communication.
questions.answer: Television and newspapers.

questions.question: Why is international trade important for a country?
questions.answer: It allows countries to exchange goods and earn foreign exchange.

questions.question: What does a favourable balance of trade mean?
questions.answer: Exports are greater than imports.

questions.question: What does an unfavourable balance of trade indicate?
questions.answer: Imports exceed exports.

questions.question: Name one important import of India.
questions.answer: Petroleum crude.

questions.question: Which sector has increased India's export earnings significantly?
questions.answer: Information Technology sector.

questions.question: Why is tourism considered a trade?
questions.answer: Because it earns foreign exchange and generates employment.

questions.question: How does tourism promote cultural exchange?
questions.answer: It allows people from different regions and cultures to interact.

questions.question: Name one example of heritage tourism in India.
questions.answer: Visiting historical monuments like the Taj Mahal.

questions.question: Why are transport and communication called lifelines of the national economy?
questions.answer: Because they support trade, movement and economic activities across the country.
